items.title: Mistral shipped a proof-focused open model
items.oneliner: Mistral's 6B Leanstral 1.5 solved 587 PutnamBench problems, pushing formal verification closer to everyday engineering.

items.title: AI vulnerability discovery is accelerating sharply
items.oneliner: June logged about 1,500 CVEs and a 3x severity spike, suggesting autonomous vuln hunting is already changing defense timelines.

items.title: Pegasus hit an EU spyware investigator
items.oneliner: A European Parliament member probing spyware was hacked with Pegasus, turning oversight itself into a surveillance target.

items.title: KDE Plasma sandboxing was bypassed
items.oneliner: A Flatpak app could escape KDE Plasma's sandbox via app spoofing, exposing how thin desktop isolation still is.

items.title: Guix disclosed severe update-chain flaws
items.oneliner: Guix warned of remote privilege escalation and file disclosure bugs in substitute and pull, making upgrades immediately non-optional.

items.title: MSI Center exposed easy SYSTEM access
items.oneliner: Preinstalled MSI software let authenticated users gain SYSTEM privileges in seconds, another reminder that OEM utilities are attack surface.

items.title: Databricks wants Postgres on object storage
items.oneliner: Lakebase externalizes Postgres storage to Parquet on S3, collapsing OLTP and analytics into one copy of data.

items.title: AMD inference economics are getting real
items.oneliner: Wafer says AMD MI355X loses on polish but wins on performance per dollar, which matters more as inference demand explodes.

items.title: Running strong local models got practical
items.oneliner: A detailed build guide claims roughly $2,000 now buys a capable local LLM box, lowering the floor for private inference.

items.title: A Windows kernel booted in-browser
items.oneliner: A custom 4MB Windows kernel now boots in a browser tab, hinting at lighter sandboxes and stranger debugging workflows.
